title: Assignment 01
text:
import platform
import psutil

print("Operating System:", platform.system(), platform.release())
print("Python Version:", platform.python_version())
print("Machine:", platform.machine())
print("Processor:", platform.processor())

mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print("Total Memory (MB):", round(mem.total / (1024**2), 2))
print("Available Memory (MB):", round(mem.available / (1024**2), 2))

Operating System: Linux 6.14.0-1017-azure
Python Version: 3.12.12
Machine: x86_64
Processor: x86_64
Total Memory (MB): 15990.48
Available Memory (MB): 14497.5

title: Research
text:
My research focuses on developing robust and efficient machine learning methods with real-world applicability. Specific areas of interest include:

Representation Learning – learning compact, transferable feature representations from limited labelled data.
Uncertainty Quantification – building models that know what they do not know.
Scientific Machine Learning – applying ML to accelerate discovery in physics, biology, and materials science.
Explainability & Fairness – ensuring models are interpretable and unbiased.

title: Nakul Padalkar
text:
PhD Student · Researcher · Educator
I am a researcher passionate about pushing the frontiers of knowledge and sharing that journey through teaching and collaboration. My work sits at the intersection of machine learning, data science, and applied research.
Learn About Me View My Research

title: Nakul Padalkar
text:
Latest News

March 2024 – New paper submitted to a top-tier conference on deep learning for scientific data.
January 2024 – Started teaching Introduction to Machine Learning (Spring 2024 semester).
October 2023 – Presented a poster at the annual departmental research symposium.

title: Nakul Padalkar
text:
Research Highlights



🧠 Machine Learning
Developing novel algorithms for sample-efficient learning and robust generalisation in real-world settings.



📊 Data Science
Building end-to-end pipelines that transform raw data into actionable insights using Python and reproducible workflows.



🔬 Applied Research
Collaborating with domain experts to apply state-of-the-art techniques in science, engineering, and social impact applications.

title: Teaching
text:
I believe that good teaching is about making complex ideas accessible and inspiring curiosity. My approach emphasises:

Active learning – encouraging students to engage with problems hands-on rather than passively absorbing lectures.
Clarity – breaking down difficult concepts into intuitive building blocks.
Inclusivity – creating an environment where every student feels welcome and supported.
Practical skills – bridging theory and practice so that students can apply what they learn.

title: Teaching
text:
Tutorials & Workshops

Introduction to Quarto for Reproducible Research
Workshop, March 2024
A hands-on workshop introducing Quarto as a tool for building reproducible research documents, interactive notebooks, and websites — all from a single source.
[Slides]   [Materials]


Python for Data Science – Crash Course
Tutorial, November 2023
A two-hour crash course covering NumPy, pandas, Matplotlib, and scikit-learn, targeted at researchers transitioning to Python from MATLAB or R.
[Notebook]   [Recording]

title: Teaching
text:
Resources
Below are some resources I frequently recommend to students:

📖 Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning – Bishop (2006)
📖 Deep Learning – Goodfellow, Bengio & Courville (2016)
🐍 Python Data Science Handbook – VanderPlas (freely available online)
📊 Quarto Documentation – Official Quarto guide

title: About Me
text:
Hi! I am Nakul Padalkar, a PhD student and researcher with a keen interest in machine learning, data science, and computational methods. I am driven by curiosity and a desire to make research accessible and impactful.
I received my undergraduate degree in Computer Science and subsequently pursued graduate studies focusing on applied machine learning. My academic journey has taken me across several exciting research problems and collaborative projects.
